assertions.text: Subject uses 'pg_dump:' lowercase prefix + colon
Subject is imperative mood with no trailing period and capitalized first word after the prefix
No conventional-commits prefix (fix:/feat:/chore:)
Body is paragraph prose (not bullet diff-recap)
Discussion: uses postgr.es/m/ shortener (not www.postgresql.org/message-id)
No Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer
No Signed-off-by trailer
No Backpatch-through line (master-only commit)
Explicitly rejects Co-Authored-By: Claude for upstream PG
Distinguishes lowercase Co-authored-by: (PG) from uppercase Co-Authored-By: (GitHub)
States attribution credits humans only, not AI tooling
Points to Author:/Reviewed-by:/Reported-by: as correct trailers
Notes names/emails are verbatim from From: header
States PG is NOT a DCO project / no Signed-off-by
Backpatch-through value is the OLDEST branch (16), not a range and not master
Format is bare major version number (e.g. '16'), not '16-18' or 'REL_16_STABLE'
Commit message is IDENTICAL across master and back branches
Mentions release-note tooling rationale for identical messages
Diagnosed-by is named as a real PG trailer
Reviewers are one-per-line, NOT comma-separated
Backpatch-through line goes last in trailer block

notes: with_skill answers are strong across all 3 evals — SKILL.md as written already covers every assertion.
Baseline fails on PG-specific points: conventional-commits prefix, archives URL form, Claude trailer, Signed-off-by, range Backpatch format, comma-separated reviewers.

Minor gaps in SKILL.md (improvement candidates for iter-2): does not call out that Discussion: also appears in the wild with http:// (saw 'http://postgr.es/m/' in b1901e2895e); does not explicitly say what to do when committer IS sole author (omit Author: line — currently only implied in the table); Author: example placeholder is awkward — could include 'omit if you are sole author' guidance inline; no explicit reminder that two spaces follow a sentence-ending period in body (PG convention visible in db5ed03217b 'assumed.  However,').
